rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ASSET SECURITY CONSULTING, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Approval For Verified Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights Pursuant To California Insurance Code 10134 Et Seq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 19, 2024, line 1, PETITIONER ASSET SECURITY CONSULTING, LLC'S Notice Of Hearing For Approval For Verified Petition For Approval For Transfer Of Structured Settlement Payment Rights Pursuant To California Insurance Code 10134 Et Seq. Hearing required; payee to appear in person or via Zoom. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KINASHA BROWN VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 19, 2024, line 10, 2 - DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens Off calendar as duplicate entry. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: KINASHA BROWN VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Yakov Wiegmann In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 19, 2024, line 10, 1 - DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s "motion to dismiss or stay action based on forum non conveniens" is denied without prejudice. The case involves an alleged assault by an Uber driver on a passenger. "On a motion for forum non conveniens, the defendant, as the moving party, bears the burden of proof." (Stangvek v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 751.) Uber relies on its counsel's declaration, which is largely inadmissible and unavailing. For example, Uber (1) fails to establish the business records exception for records; (2) fails to establish that any witness would be inconvenienced by litigation in San Francisco; (3) offers only information and belief that the Uber driver resided in Nevada. (Wiegmann Dec. 1:1-2:4.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY MOLNO VS. DAGEN MINISTERO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Second Amended Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 473(A)(1); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Lawrence Mann In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 19, 2024, line 8, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY MOLNO'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Second Amended Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 473(A)(1) Plaintiff's unopposed motion to file a second amended complaint is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ZACHARY LOUDEN VS. ATLASSIAN CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 19, 2024, line 9, DEFENDANTS ATLASSIAN CORPORATION, ATLASSIAN US, INC.'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend. Even by California's liberal pleading standards, the complaint is too skimpy. For example, the 14-line "factual background" is actually a laundry list of legal concepts - bereft of facts and names - that could apply to most any employment case. (Cmplt. 2:22-3:9; CCP 425.10(a)(1) ["statement of facts" required].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)
